title: wyred contract v0 — standalone allocation/lock/escalation records (.records.json)
description: Descriptive schema for the standalone records data path, extracted from its producer wyred/paths.py `build_records` — there is NO prose (F1). Twelve keys are always emitted (allocation/artifact/bindings/bound_parts/connector_pinout/escalations/path/pool_spares/resolutions/series/solver_version/stamp); `forked_from` is the ONE optional key (added only when the l1 doc carries a series-fork record — in ga019 only watchy_v1_revb). `path` is const "records". F4: `forked_from` here is the {series, reason} OBJECT (common.defs forked_from_record), NOT the bare string the lifecycle artifact uses. F7: `escalations` are the RICH objects (same shape as the l1 escalations, copied verbatim from the l1 doc), NOT the l2 string array. `resolutions` maps a demand-id to a "rail:|bus:|role:" target; `pool_spares` maps a pool to its free-unit index array. additionalProperties:false throughout.

bindings.description: map <role-id> -> <refdes> for singly-bound roles (== the alloc artifact's bindings).

bound_parts.description: map <role-id> -> array of realized part rows (== the alloc artifact's bound_parts).

connector_pinout.description: external-interface ICD rows (== the alloc artifact's connector_pinout); [] when the design declares no connectors.

escalations.description: F7: the RICH escalation objects sourced verbatim from the l1 doc's escalations (build_records copies l1["escalations"]). The l2 graph's same-named field is a plain string array instead. [] when the resolve produced no escalations.

resolutions.description: map <demand-id> -> the resolved target, a "<kind>:<name>" string where <kind> is rail | bus | role (from l1 attrs.resolutions). Pattern-constrained to those three prefixes.

additionalProperties.$comment:
Regex-engine caveat (shared-oracle blind spot): in Python `re`, `$` also matches just before a single trailing '
' and `.` never matches '
', so a value like 'rail:VBAT
' MATCHES here but would be REJECTED by an ECMAScript engine (ajv), whose `$` anchors only at the very end. The goldens carry no trailing-newline resolution targets, so the two oracles agree on the corpus; a stricter '^(rail|bus|role):[^
]+\z' (Python) / '...$' with no trailing newline (ECMA) would remove the divergence if ever needed.

pool_spares.description: map <pool> -> array of that pool's free (unallocated) unit indices (from l1 attrs.pool_spares). {} when no pool has spares.

escalation.description: A ladder-rung-4 escalation WITH explanation (F7), identical in shape to the l1 escalation (schema_l1._escalation_to_json): code/msg always emitted; subjects/conflict/relaxation emit-only-when-non-empty. Defined locally to keep the records schema self-contained (the l1 schema defines the same shape independently).
